title: Tetractomia lanceolata
intro: Tetractomia lanceolata, also known as the lance-leaved Tetractomia, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Tetractomia lanceolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Tetractomia lanceolata is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment to treat various health ailments, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmacological studies.
